findings.evidence: PR line 145 defines `ratBelow`; Mathlib documents and names the same object as `absNorm (under ℤ I)` with `Int.absNorm_under_eq_sInf`, `Int.absNorm_under_dvd_absNorm`, and `Nat.absNorm_under_prime` in `Mathlib/RingTheory/Ideal/Int.lean:23-30,70-121`.
findings.fix: Rename the helper and dependent theorem names to Mathlib terminology, e.g. `absNormUnder` and `under_eq_of_absNorm_under_eq`, or inline `Ideal.absNorm (Ideal.under ℤ P)` if the abbreviation is not meant as API.
findings.issue: `ratBelow` names `Ideal.absNorm (Ideal.under ℤ P)` using new nonstandard terminology and the abbreviation `rat`; Mathlib already uses `under`/`absNorm_under` for this object.

findings.evidence: The theorem at lines 207-209 has hypotheses `(hr : ratBelow F P = ratBelow F Q)` and `(hc : primeCoord F P = primeCoord F Q)`, while `primeCoord` is only an index in `primesOverFinset (Ideal.under ℤ P)` at lines 147-150.
findings.fix: Rename it to include both key hypotheses, e.g. `prime_eq_of_ratBelow_eq_of_primeCoord_eq` or, after the terminology fix above, `prime_eq_of_absNormUnder_eq_of_primeCoord_eq`.
findings.issue: `prime_eq_of_coord_eq` overstates the theorem: equal coordinates alone do not determine the prime, since the coordinate index is reused for each rational prime; the statement also needs equality of `ratBelow`.

summary: Two exported names should be fixed before merging: one introduces nonstandard terminology for an existing Mathlib concept, and one theorem name overstates the information used to prove equality.
